query: Which enable strategy is recommended and what does it require?
expected_behavior: Must read references/enable-strategies.md. Answer: Approach A — gate ProfilerModule with ConditionalModule.registerWhen(..., isProfilerEnabled) and pair it with ProfilerNoopModule as the fallback, so the active module is never loaded when off. It requires @nestjs/config, because ConditionalModule lives there. Both approaches keep ProfilerService injectable.

Detect that @nestjs/config is NOT present, so ConditionalModule (Approach A) is unavailable. Either offer to install @nestjs/config to unlock Approach A, or use Approach B: ProfilerModule.forRoot({ isGlobal: true, enabled: process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production' }). Must NOT silently use ConditionalModule.registerWhen without @nestjs/config. Install core with npm (package-lock.json detected): 'npm install @eleven-labs/nest-profiler nestjs-cls'.

query: I don't use @nestjs/config. How can I still turn the profiler off in production?
expected_behavior: Must read references/enable-strategies.md. Answer: use Approach B, the synchronous top-level 'enabled' flag: ProfilerModule.forRoot({ isGlobal: true, enabled: process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production' }). When enabled is false the core registers only an inert no-op layer. Note: enabled is NOT resolved inside a forRootAsync useFactory — it stays a top-level key.

query: Do I need a no-op module for each optional collector like I do for the core?
expected_behavior: Must read references/enable-strategies.md. Answer: No. Only the core needs the ProfilerNoopModule fallback. Collectors need no no-op counterpart — they self-register through discovery and do nothing when the active profiler is absent; just gate them with the same condition.
